mechanism_narrative: Declining federal funds rates reduce the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly increasing present equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns during rate cuts are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 4.5 percent, labor market slack signals weakening consumer demand and corporate earnings pressure, triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns within one week.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite negative signal, or monthly out-of-sample accuracy falls below 55%.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous decline in retail sales and housing starts indicates a broad contraction in consumer and investment demand, which reduces aggregate corporate revenue and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Joint decline in retail sales and housing starts fails to predict negative quarterly returns, with Rank IC < 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: During late-cycle expansions, a sustained drop in the industrial production index reflects capacity utilization exhaustion and supply-side bottlenecks, which compresses profit margins and generates negative S&P 500 forward returns over one month.
failure_condition: Industrial production declines during late-cycle expansions do not precede negative monthly returns, or the signal's predictive power vanishes when tested outside the late-cycle regime.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate rises above 4.0 percent, higher borrowing costs suppress corporate leverage and capex spending, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns within one week.
failure_condition: SPX generates positive 5-day returns in > 65% of instances when FEDFUNDS exceeds 4.0%, or the threshold signal shows no statistical significance in out-of-sample backtests.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising total nonfarm payrolls and a simultaneously rising unemployment rate signals labor force participation deterioration and hidden economic weakness, forecasting negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The payroll-unemployment divergence fails to predict negative quarterly returns, with a rolling Rank IC consistently below 0.01 over 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Higher real GDP growth directly increases corporate earnings expectations and equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month as capital markets price in stronger economic activity.
failure_condition: GDP growth rates show no statistically significant correlation with 20-day forward returns, or the signal's Rank IC remains below 0.03 across all tested rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: When the industrial production index falls below 105.0, it indicates a sharp contraction in manufacturing output and supply chain stress, triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns within one week.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite the negative signal, or the threshold condition fails to improve out-of-sample classification accuracy above 50%.
